title: scaphochlamys subbiloba
intro: Scaphochlamys subbiloba, also known as the Malay gold, is a species of flowering plant in the family Zingiberaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The health benefits of Scaphochlamys subbiloba include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues. It can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for consumption, and it is also used in traditional cooking and as a garnish in various dishes.
